rationale: Determinism check requires `post1_self_.epmap == post2_self_.epmap` (struct equality on `HashMap<CAckState>`, which is declared `#[verifier(external_body)]`). The only postcondition of `cack_state_swap` is `HashMap::swap_spec(...)`, which constrains the views (`Map<AbstractEndPoint, V>`) but says nothing about the underlying opaque HashMap struct. I verified empirically (with a minimal test case under the same Verus toolchain) that view equality on an `external_body` struct does NOT imply `==` (nor `=~=`, nor `=~~=`) on the struct itself. Verus carries the opaque value through SMT as uninterpreted; without an axiom connecting `a@ == b@` to `a == b`, the implication cannot be discharged. The ack-state and tuple conjuncts of the equality predicate ARE provable (the swap_spec's `match get_spec(...)` arm pins both `post1_ack_state` and `post2_ack_state` to the same `CAckState` witness via SMT transitivity, giving struct equality and hence per-field equality including the `Vec<CSingleMessag
